title: Inventory satellite smoke-detection, chemical-transport model, ground-monitor, and respiratory health data sources for fire-prone regions
outcome: A dated source inventory that separates satellite smoke-plume products (NOAA HMS), chemical-transport model composition (GEOS-Chem), ground PM2.5 monitors, and respiratory health-utilization data into usable, limited, and rejected categories with explicit LMIC coverage assessment.
inputs: GEOS-Chem model documentation and validation studies
Ground PM2.5 monitor network inventories for fire-prone regions
Published systematic reviews of wildfire smoke health impacts
Health management information system documentation for LMIC fire-prone regions
expected_artifact: datasets.md update plus evidence.json records for each accepted source.
validation_method: Reviewer checks each listed source for URL, date, access status, license, spatial resolution, temporal coverage, and documented limitations — particularly LMIC data availability and satellite detection gaps under cloud cover.
done_condition: At least five candidate data sources are classified as usable, limited, or rejected with explicit reasons covering resolution, coverage, LMIC availability, and known detection gaps.

title: Validate satellite-derived smoke exposure against ground-monitor PM2.5 in at least two fire-prone regions
outcome: A validation report documenting the correlation between NOAA HMS smoke-plume indicators, GEOS-Chem smoke-specific PM2.5 estimates, and ground-level PM2.5 monitors during smoke and non-smoke periods for at least two regions with different fire regimes, with explicit documentation of monitor density and LMIC coverage gaps.
inputs: Ground PM2.5 monitor data from EPA (US), state agencies, and available LMIC networks
Meteorological data for atmospheric context during smoke events
expected_artifact: validation.md update with satellite-to-monitor correlation methodology, regional comparison, and LMIC gap documentation.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the exposure validation on a different time period and confirms the correlation direction and magnitude hold for each region, with explicit documentation of which monitors were used and where coverage was insufficient.
done_condition: Validation report includes satellite-to-monitor correlation coefficients for at least two regions, smoke vs. non-smoke seasonal comparison, monitor density assessment, and explicit LMIC coverage-gap documentation.

title: Analyze respiratory health impact of smoke-specific PM2.5 with confound controls for non-smoke exposure
outcome: A health-impact analysis linking validated smoke-specific PM2.5 exposure to respiratory health outcomes for at least one region, with explicit confound controls for non-smoke PM2.5, temperature, humidity, and baseline respiratory disease seasonality.
expected_artifact: outputs.md update with health-impact methodology, effect estimates, and confound documentation.
validation_method: Replicator reruns the health-impact model with alternative confound specifications and confirms the smoke-specific effect estimate is robust to confound choice, with explicit documentation of which health data was available and LMIC evidence gaps.
done_condition: Health-impact analysis includes effect estimates with confidence intervals, confound controls for at least three non-smoke factors, sensitivity analysis, and explicit documentation of LMIC evidence gaps and data limitations.

title: Red-team smoke-exposure misclassification and health-impact overstatement risks
outcome: A failure-mode review identifying how satellite-derived smoke exposure could misclassify exposure, how health-impact estimates could overstate effects by ignoring confounders, and how results could be misused to justify panic-based fire suppression or undermine prescribed-burning programs.
expected_artifact: outputs.md and SAFETY.md linked review note.
validation_method: Reviewer confirms every health-impact claim has documented exposure-validation limitations, confound controls, and LMIC evidence gaps. Second reviewer replicates the failure-mode check.
done_condition: Every smoke-health claim is either blocked, softened to analytic language with explicit exposure-misclassification caveats, or gated behind domain review and replicated red-team review.

title: Check whether outputs match air-quality management and health-ministry decision needs
outcome: A field-facing review of whether the smoke-health analysis would change any real decision for air-quality management agencies, health-ministry environmental health units, or emergency management offices without creating panic or undermining prescribed-burning programs.
expected_artifact: playbooks.md update with use and non-use guidance for air-quality and health-ministry audiences.
validation_method: Field reviewer identifies the actual decision, user, timing, and misuse risk — particularly the risk that health-impact estimates cause panic during fire events or are used to justify blanket fire suppression that increases catastrophic fire risk. Second reviewer replicates the non-use-case check.
done_condition: The output has a named user (e.g., state air-quality manager), named decision (e.g., smoke advisory threshold), explicit non-use cases (e.g., not for individual evacuation orders, not for prescribed-burning ban justification), and replicated field-reality review.
